title: Coelogyne hamata
intro: Coelogyne hamata is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Coelogyne, a group of terrestrial and epiphytic plants known for their medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne hamata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne hamata is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and fever.
taxonomy: Coelogyne hamata is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This genus, Coelogyne, is a group of terrestrial and epiphytic orchids native to tropical regions of Asia. The Coelogyne genus is further divided into several subgenera, with C. hamata belonging to the subgenus Coelogyne. The species C. hamata is a rare and lesser-known member of the genus, found in specific regions of Southeast Asia. Its classification as a terrestrial orchid is due to its growth habit on or near the ground.
